# RQ-0950 Phase 1 Plan - CI Output Passthrough Before Compliance Message

## Task Goal
Guarantee that every agent-facing CI failure continue message includes actionable CI output context from both the start and end of the CI log before compliance/enforcement instructions, so early failures (format/lint/parse) are visible without rerunning CI manually.

## Why
The CI pipeline can fail before tests. Tail-only snippets hide first-failure root causes (for example Ruff/TOML parse failures), causing retry loops and wasted runs. The continue-session supervision flow must surface enough context immediately in the compliance message.

## Current State (from repo inspection)
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/ci.rs` already contains:
1. `format_ci_output_for_message(...)` (head+tail formatter; stderr-first merge)
2. `strict_ci_gate_compliance_message(...)` (compliance message composition)
3. `run_ci_gate_with_continue_session(...)` (auto-retry + escalation control flow)
- Continue-session call chain entrypoints:
1. `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/shared.rs`
2. `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/mod.rs`
3. `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/parallel_worker.rs`
- Existing tests already cover formatter/compliance basics; refinement is needed to prevent branch-specific regressions.

## Primary Regression Risk
Auto-retry paths appear to use `strict_ci_gate_compliance_message`, but escalation/manual-continue branches can diverge. Any divergence can send an agent response without the canonical CI snippet.

## Implementation Directive (Phase 2)

### 1. Keep one canonical formatter contract in `ci.rs`
Target function: `format_ci_output_for_message(stdout, stderr, max_head_lines, max_tail_lines)`.

Required invariants:
- stderr lines before stdout lines
- show head + tail when truncated
- include total lines, explicit shown ranges, omitted count
- full passthrough when under budget
- explicit fallback when no output

Use this structure (adapt to existing local names):

```rust
fn format_ci_output_for_message(
    stdout: &str,
    stderr: &str,
    max_head_lines: usize,
    max_tail_lines: usize,
) -> String {
    let mut lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();
    lines.extend(stderr.lines());
    lines.extend(stdout.lines());

    let total_lines = lines.len();
    if total_lines == 0 {
        return "No CI output was captured from stdout/stderr.".to_string();
    }

    let display_budget = max_head_lines.saturating_add(max_tail_lines);
    if total_lines <= display_budget {
        return format!(
            "CI output ({} lines):\n```text\n{}\n```",
            total_lines,
            lines.join("\n")
        );
    }

    let head: Vec<&str> = lines.iter().take(max_head_lines).copied().collect();
    let tail_start = total_lines.saturating_sub(max_tail_lines);
    let tail: Vec<&str> = lines.iter().skip(tail_start).copied().collect();
    let omitted = total_lines.saturating_sub(head.len() + tail.len());

    let head_range = format!("1-{}", head.len());
    let tail_range = format!("{}-{}", tail_start + 1, total_lines);

    format!(
        "CI output ({} lines total; showing lines {} and {}):\n\
```text\n{}\n```\n\
\n... {} lines omitted ...\n\
\n```text\n{}\n```",
        total_lines,
        head_range,
        tail_range,
        head.join("\n"),
        omitted,
        tail.join("\n")
    )
}
```

### 2. Preserve strict message ordering in `strict_ci_gate_compliance_message`
The composed agent message must keep this sequence:
1. CI gate + exit code summary
2. formatted CI output snippet
3. detected-pattern guidance (if any)
4. compliance/enforcement text

Canonical shape:

```rust
let output_snippet = format_ci_output_for_message(&result.stdout, &result.stderr, 50, 50);
let detected = detect_ci_error_pattern(&(result.stderr.clone() + "\n" + &result.stdout));
let specific_guidance = detected
    .map(|p| format_detected_pattern(&p))
    .unwrap_or_default();

format!(
    "CI gate ({command_display}): CI failed with exit code {exit_code_display}.\n\n\
{output_snippet}\n\n\
{specific_guidance}\
Fix the errors above before continuing.\n\n\
COMMON PATTERNS:\n..."
)
```

### 3. Eliminate branch divergence in continue-session failure messaging
In `run_ci_gate_with_continue_session`, prebuild one strict message and reuse it across all agent-facing failure branches (auto-retry and manual/escalation continue paths).

Pattern to apply:

```rust
let strict_message = strict_ci_gate_compliance_message(&resolved_command, &gate_result);

if continue_session.ci_failure_retry_count < CI_GATE_AUTO_RETRY_LIMIT {
    resume_continue_session(
        continue_session,
        &strict_message,
        true,
        Some(ci_failure_pattern.clone()),
    )?;
    continue;
}

// If escalation/manual continue still returns a message to the agent,
// include strict_message first so CI output context is never dropped.
let escalation_message = format!(
    "{}\n\nCI gate has failed repeatedly with the same error pattern. Manual intervention is required.",
    strict_message
);
```

If current branch behavior intentionally does not resume agent sessions on a terminal path, keep termination behavior unchanged; only ensure any agent-directed message path includes the canonical snippet.

### 4. Expand/tighten regression tests in `ci.rs`
Do not duplicate equivalent tests; strengthen existing assertions where present.

Required coverage:
1. Large output head+tail ranges include earliest and latest lines plus omitted metadata.
2. Small output shows full content with no omitted marker.
3. Empty stdout/stderr emits explicit fallback text.
4. Compliance message includes formatted snippet before enforcement text.
5. Escalation/manual-continue agent-message path (if present) still includes formatted CI snippet.

Example assertion skeletons:

```rust
assert!(msg.contains("CI output ("));
assert!(msg.contains("showing lines"));
assert!(msg.contains("lines omitted"));
assert!(msg.contains("err-1"));
assert!(msg.contains("out-200"));
```

```rust
let out_idx = msg.find("CI output (").unwrap();
let enforce_idx = msg.find("Fix the errors above before continuing.").unwrap();
assert!(out_idx < enforce_idx);
```

### 5. Blast-radius validation (no unnecessary rewrites)
Confirm no alternate compliance-message formatter exists in:
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/mod.rs`
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/parallel_worker.rs`
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/phases/shared.rs`
- `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/logging.rs`

If an alternate path exists, route it to the same canonical message builder in `ci.rs`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent-facing CI compliance messages always include head+tail CI context before enforcement guidance.
- Truncated outputs clearly report total lines, shown ranges, and omitted count.
- Under-budget outputs are fully shown.
- No continue-session message branch bypasses the canonical snippet when communicating CI failure to the agent.
- Regression tests cover formatter and message-order behavior.

## Phase 2 Verification
Run after implementation:
- `cargo test -p ralph`

Optional targeted iteration first:
- `cargo test -p ralph supervision::ci`

## Executor Autonomy
This directive is authoritative. If symbol names or file locations differ, adapt the same behavioral invariants to the real code paths. Prioritize correctness of agent-visible CI diagnostics over literal snippet matching.
